tweets.text:
Someone found a way to get Claude Opus 4.7 for FREE. Amazon's coding platform is giving away a full month of Pro Plus - 2,000 credits, zero charge.

The platform is Kura - Amazon's AI coding tool. Web interface, IDE like Cursor, and CLI like Claude Code. One month free on any paid plan, including Pro Plus at $40.

> Here's the exact flow:

Go to ko.()dev -> create account via Google or GitHub -> hit "Upgrade to unlock plus" -> select Pro Plus ($40 plan) -> promo applies at checkout -> $0 charged -> card required but nothing debited.

You get access to Claude Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6, and Opus 4.6. Up to 2,000 credits for the month.

The CLI demo shows Claude Opus 4.7 building a full developer landing page from a single prompt - generates the code, creates the files, spins up a local server. 

Same output you'd get from Claude Code Max at $100/month.

Cancel before the trial ends if you dont want to pay. Settings -> Manage Plan -> Cancel.

This promotion can end at any time. The window is open right now.

article.title: Opus 4.7, GPT 5.5 and GLM 5.1 for free via Tembo
article.preview_text:
20 requests per day. No payment. No card.
Cursor Pro costs $20/mo, Claude Code Max starts at $100. Tembo gives you the same models for free: 20 tokens per day per account. The limit covers Opus 4.7,
